data.title: Philadelphia Fed's Harker still sees rates on downward path
data.description:
Philadelphia Fed Patrick Harker says he still sees the Federal Open Market Committee on a downward policy rate path. But the economy remains "in very unsettled times." There remain "numerous upside risks," he added, according to his prepared remarks.

As the FOMC's minutes from its last meeting described, the Fed officials are likely to be more cautious in their easing. "The exact speed I continue to go along this path will be fully dependent upon the incoming data," Harker said in his prepared text.

He's optimistic about the U.S. economy. "The overall underpinnings of our economy remain strong," he said. He's particularly encouraged that "shelter disinflation has finally enmeshed itself into the monthly readings after being the upside outlier for many, many months."

Tempering that optimism is an increase in mortgage delinquencies, he added.

Harker, who will retire from the Philly Fed on June 30, 2025, is not a voting member of the FOMC this year.

The delivered remarks will be followed by a Q&A segment.

Breaking news... check back for updates.

data.title: BP to boost oil output from India's largest field by 44%, ONGC says
data.description:
BP (NYSE:BP) plans to increase oil production at India's largest field by 44% and gas production by 89%, state-controlled Oil & Natural Gas Corp. said Thursday.

The comments came a day after ONGC selected BP (BP) as its technical service provider to help boost production at its flagship Mumbai High field from a baseline of 45.47M metric tons of crude and 70.4B cm of gas.

BP (BP) plans to raise oil production by 44% to 65.41M tons and gas output by 89% to 112.63B cm from the Mumbai High asset beginning in the next fiscal year starting April 1, with full-scale visibility anticipated by 2027-28, ONGC said.

The incremental production is forecast to generate up to $10.3B of additional oil and gas revenue for India, and contributions from royalties and other levies totaling as much as $5B, the company said.

The Mumbai High field off India's west coast reached peak production of 471K bbl/day of oil in March 1985, but declined to 134K bbl/day in April 2024, a tender document showed last year.

data.title: Italian PM Meloni denies discussing Starlink deal with Musk
data.description:
Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Thursday refuted claims that she has discussed any potential deal for encrypted communications to be used in the country's military with Starlink (STRLK) (SPACE) owner Elon Musk.

Meloni has maintained a favorable relationship and met with Musk during a surprise visit to Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence over the weekend ahead of Trump's second presidential term.

However, she has strongly rejected claims of reaching a telecommunication services deal and said she is only evaluating options.

"The prime minister's office categorically denies, considering it ridiculous, that SpaceX was a topic during the meeting with U.S. President-elect Donald Trump," the Italian PM's office said.

"We are simply in the investigative phase, which is why I don't understand all the accusations that have been made. I'll tell you more: I don't even personally have clear ideas on this matter," Meloni reportedly said at a press conference.

A Bloomberg report from Sunday suggested Italy was in talks for a $1.65B deal with Starlink.

Musk on Monday posted on his platform X that he was "ready to provide Italy the most secure and advanced connectivity!"

Italy's defense minister, Guido Crosetto, told parliament on Wednesday his office had not signed or agreed on any contracts with Musk's company.

Starlink, a unit of SpaceX, currently has 6,700 low-Earth orbit satellites active in space, nearly two-thirds of all active satellites in place. Last year, it added more than 20 countries to its customer base, providing services in more than 100 countries and territories.

data.title: CleanSpark surpasses over 10,000 bitcoins in treasury
data.description:
• CleanSpark (NASDAQ:CLSK) has surpassed over 10,000-bitcoin mark held in its treasury, representing a 236% year-over-year growth in the reserve.
  • The miner now holds 10,097 bitcoins in its treasury, all self-mined exclusively from its U.S.-based mining operations.

data.title: Novo, Eli Lilly to dominate global pharma sales in 2025: report
data.description:
Blockbuster weight loss medications from Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) lead Evaluate Pharma’s list of best-selling drugs projected for 2025 after the incretins pushed Bristol Myers’ (BMY) cancer medication Opdivo from the 10-product list.

The GLP-1s semaglutide and tirzepatide marketed by NVO and LLY as Ozempic/Wegovy and Mounjaro/Zepbound, respectively, for type 2 diabetes and weight loss, are expected to generate more than $70B in sales collectively this year, the pharma research group said, citing consensus forecasts.

The popular injectables also dominate the list of the fastest-growing medications in 2025. “For Novo and Lilly, one of their main priorities in 2025 will be keeping up with demand and meeting expectations of both patients and investors,” Evaluate analysts wrote.

For example, LLY’s Zepbound sales are projected to double from the prior year to $11.3B after the U.S. FDA approved the dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist for obesity and obstructive sleep apnea in 2023 and 2024, respectively.

As for branded products, Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) cancer blockbuster dominates the list, and the checkpoint inhibitor is on track to generate peak sales in 2025, Evaluate Pharma projects. The New Jersey-based firm’s plans to seek a regulatory nod for an injectable version next year are set to extend the franchise’s long-term prospects before the drug's U.S. market exclusivity ends in 2028.

Meanwhile, NVO and LLY’s GLP-1 franchise has pushed Keytruda rival Opdivo from the top ten list for the first time in several years.

Bristol Myers (BMY) of Princeton, New Jersey is advancing a similar injectable version developed with drug technology firm Halozyme (HALO). Late last year, the FDA approved the product branded as Opdivo Qvantig for most previously approved Opdivo indications.

Evaluate’s top-selling brand-name drugs for 2025 are as follows:

  1. Keytruda: Merck (NYSE:MRK)
  2. Ozempic: Novo Nordisk (NVO)
  3. Mounjaro: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY)
  4. Dupixent: Regeneron (NASDAQ:REGN)/ Sanofi (SNY)
  5. Skyrizi: AbbVie (ABBV)
  6. Eliquis: Bristol Myers (BMY)/ Pfizer (PFE)
  7. Biktavy: Gilead (GILD)
  8. Wegovy: Novo Nordisk (NVO)
  9. Darzalex: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ)
  10. Zepbound: Eli Lilly (LLY)

data.title: Med-X enters distribution agreement with Anasac Ambiental
data.description:
• Dazed, formerly known as Med-X (OTC:MEDH) announced an international distribution agreement with Anasac Ambiental, providing exclusive Nature-Cide distribution rights in Chile, Argentina, Peru and Colombia.
  • Anasac is a Chilean company providing sustainable solutions and will serve as the exclusive distributor of the Nature-Cide portfolio of indoor and outdoor pest control solutions.
  • "As a major agricultural region with significant pest pressure, the need for Nature-Cide in home, agricultural and commercial applications has never been stronger. We look forward to working closely with the Anasac team to provide the Nature-Cide product portfolio as a best-in-class option to pest control operators seeking sustainable natural and effective solutions in these exciting new international markets," Matthew Mills, CEO of Med-X said.

data.title: Fertilizer market looks stable for 2025, RBC analysts say
data.description:
Fertilizer prices this year are likely to be steady as crop profitability returns to historical levels, analysts at RBC Capital Marketers said in a report Wednesday. This dynamic is generally positive for investors in makers of farming chemicals, they said.

“Fertilizer equities provide good cash generation at reasonable valuations and defensive commodity exposure against broader macro risk,” Andrew Wong, analyst at RBC, said in a January 8 report.

RBC commentary:

  • CF Industries (CF) rated Outperform, $100 price target: “A best-in-class operator with strong cash flows and a constructive nitrogen outlook.”
  • Nutrien (NYSE:NTR) rated Outperform, $60 price target: “Could see a moderate re-rate, on better operations and steady ag/fertilizer fundamentals.”
  • LSB Industries (LXU) rated Outperform, $10 price target: “We see a transition year for LSB Industries…setting up for a bigger 2026.
  • Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) rated Sector Perform, $27 price target: “We are neutral on Mosaic, with potential upside if phosphate prices stay elevated and ops improve, but execution risks.”

data.title: Activist investor pushes for new leadership, board overhaul at Air Products
data.description:
Activist investor Mantle Ridge ramps up its call for a shakeup at Air Products (NYSE:APD), sending a letter to shareholders Thursday saying the company has "dramatically underperformed over the past five years relative to its peers and its potential, a direct result of the poor stewardship" of Chairman and CEO Seifi Ghasemi and the board.

The letter is the latest exchange between the company and Mantle Ridge, which holds a $1.3B stake in Air Products (APD) and is proposing to replace Ghasemi as CEO with former Linde executive Eduardo Menezes, in addition to four new directors.

Mantle Ridge emphasized Menezes' three decades of experience at Linde, where he held senior regional roles, but Air Products (APD) said Menezes lacks public company CEO experience.

The company said Dennis Reilley, one of Mantle Ridge's proposed directors, is alleged to have leaked confidential information from three public company boards while a member of those boards.

The company said it thoroughly vetted Mantle Ridge's director nominees through a leading national search firm, and found them "inferior" to its current directors and new nominees.

The heated exchanges with Mantle Ridge follows scrutiny from another investor, D.E. Shaw, which recently expressed "continued disappointment" with Air Products (APD) in managing an effective CEO succession process.

data.title: LA wildfires cost to rank among worst US disasters - report
data.description:
A preliminary estimate of the Los Angeles wildfires are likely the costliest U.S. wildfire ever and puts the disaster among the nation's most costly natural disasters, according to a media report. At least five people have died from the fires.

Accuweather Inc. estimates the likely cost at $52B-$57B in damage and economic loss, Bloomberg reported on Thursday. After a very dry past few months, combined with the Santa Ana winds, the fires are spreading into neighborhoods, destroying thousands of homes in densely populated southern California and forcing the evacuation of more than 100,000 residents.

The U.S.'s most expensive natural disaster was Hurricane Katrina in 2005, with an estimated cost of $200B, according to National Oceanic and Atmosphere Administration data dating to 1980. The costliest wildfires event was the Western wildfires in 2018 at an estimated cost of $30B.

In addition to the deaths and property damage caused by the Los Angeles wildfires, health impacts due to the inhalation of smoke are likely to have long-lasting effects and the region's tourism business will be hurt, AccuWeather said, according to the report.

data.title: AIG appoints Christopher Flatt as chief underwriting officer
data.description:
• American International Group (NYSE:AIG) announced on Thursday the appointment of Christopher Flatt as chief underwriting officer, effective immediately.
  • Flatt currently serves as the head of AIG North America alternative channels.
  • Flatt, who will report directly to CEO Peter Zaffino, succeeds Christopher Schaper.
  • Schaper was appointed the chief risk officer of the multi-line insurer in November 2024.
  • Source: Press Release
